stories.title: TOMY Boon NURSH Baby Bottle Recall — Choking Hazard, 40,000 Units at Walmart
stories.angle: TOMY International recalled approximately 40,000 Boon NURSH 8 oz Reusable Baby Bottles on June 4, 2026 due to a choking hazard. The hard outer plastic shell can bubble and peel, creating loose film-like pieces that pose a choking risk to infants. Sold exclusively at Walmart and Walmart.com, Nov 2025–May 2026, approximately $20 for a 3-pack. Item number B11654. 135 reports of peeling/bubbling; no injuries. Parents should stop use immediately. Remedy: $22 store credit at booninc.com OR replacement set; contact TOMY at 866-725-4407 or recall.tomy.com/nursh.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
RECALL: TOMY Boon NURSH 8 oz baby bottles from Walmart.

Stop use. Refund or replacement at recall.tomy.com/nursh or 866-725-4407. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.text:

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: TOMY Boon NURSH Baby Bottle Recall: Choking Hazard Affects 40,000 Bottles Sold at Walmart

TOMY Boon NURSH Baby Bottle Recall

---

research.facts:
TOMY Boon NURSH Baby Bottle Recall
**Source 1:** CPSC.gov — "TOMY Recalls Boon NURSH 8 oz Reusable Baby Bottles Due to Choking Hazard; Sold at Walmart"
- Recall date: June 4, 2026
- Units: approximately 40,000
- Product: Boon NURSH 8 oz Reusable Baby Bottles 3-Pack, pink tie-dye
- Item number B11654, UPC 669028116546 (printed on packaging bottom)
- Sold exclusively at Walmart and Walmart.com, Nov 2025–May 2026, ~$20
- Hazard: outer hard plastic shell bubbles/peels → loose film-like pieces → choking risk
- Reports: 135 reports of peeling/bubbling; 0 injuries
- Action: stop use immediately; contact TOMY
- Remedy: $22 booninc.com store credit OR replacement set in different color
- Process: submit photo of bottles with "RECALL" written + certify disposal
- Contact: 866-725-4407 M-F 8am–4pm CT; cs@tomy.com; recall.tomy.com/nursh
- Confidence: HIGH (CPSC.gov primary source)
**Source 2:** foxbusiness.com — "TOMY recalls 40,000 baby bottles sold at Walmart over choking hazard"
- Cross-confirms 40,000 units, Walmart-exclusive, $20, choking hazard
- Confidence: HIGH (second independent source)
**Source 3:** wfaa.com / wgrz.com (multiple local news outlets)
- Cross-confirms same facts; no new details
- Confidence: HIGH
**Freshness justification:** Recall announced June 4, 2026. Content published June 8 — 4 days later. Still fresh and actionable for parents.

stories.title: Blodgett Open Space Trail Expansion Nearly Complete — 14 Miles of New Trails This Summer
stories.angle: The City of Colorado Springs is completing a major expansion at Blodgett Open Space — 14 miles of new and improved trails expected to be finished by summer 2026. The 384-acre open space borders Pike National Forest near the Rockrimmon area. New additions include mountain bike-only trails in the southern section and hiking/multi-use trails in the northern section, with a new trailhead and parking lot. Family-friendly lower trails (Blodgett Trail, Chickadee Trail half-mile loop) are already accessible. The Trackchair program expands this season to 2 guided hikes daily (Wed–Sun) at Blodgett among other locations.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
COS families: Blodgett Open Space is getting 14 miles of new trails this summer.

New mountain bike trails, hiking paths, and a bigger trailhead — free public open space.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

research.facts:
Blodgett Open Space Trail Expansion
**Source 1:** coloradosprings.gov/BlodgettOpenSpace (City of Colorado Springs)
- Blodgett Open Space: 384 acres
- Borders Pike National Forest
- 14 miles of new + improved trails expected complete "close to summertime" / summer 2026
- Mountain bike-only trails in southern half (Blodgett Bike Hill)
- Hiking/multi-use trails in northern half
- New trailhead + parking lot at base of old quarry
- Family-friendly lower trails: Blodgett Trail (great city views, mellow steepness), Chickadee Trail (half-mile loop)
- Confidence: HIGH (official city source)
**Source 2:** koaa.com — "Twice the trails: Work starts to expand Blodgett Open Space"
- Confirms expansion plans and trail count
- Confidence: HIGH
**Source 3:** coloradosprings.gov/news (TOPS2026 / Trackchair program)
- Trackchair program expanding to 2 guided hikes daily, Wed–Sun, at Blodgett Open Space among locations
- Confidence: HIGH
**Freshness justification:** Trail expansion announced for summer 2026 opening. Currently in final stage. Relevant as school year ends and families seek outdoor activities.

stories.title: Summer Slide Is Real — How COS Families Can Keep Kids Learning This Summer
stories.angle: Studies show kids lose 25–30% of school-year learning over summer, with math hit hardest and younger children most vulnerable. With schools out in Colorado Springs, local families face the classic summer challenge. Practical tips backed by research: 20–30 minutes of structured review 3–4 times per week, daily reading (20 min), math in everyday activities (cooking, shopping), plus local COS resources — PPYMCA summer programs, PPLD Summer Adventure (through July 31), Rock Ledge Ranch (this week through June 14). Evidence-based, local-focused guidance.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Summer slide is real — kids can lose 30% of school learning over break.

Quick fix: read 20 min daily, add math to cooking and chores.

Free COS help: PPYMCA camps, PPLD Summer Adventure through July 31. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: Summer Slide Prevention: What Colorado Springs Parents Need to Know in 2026

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Summer Slide Prevention

#### Social Image (1080x1350)
**Prompt:** A warm, inviting scene of a child lying on their stomach on a colorful blanket outdoors, reading a picture book. Soft grass background, light dappled shade from nearby trees. The child looks engaged and happy. Late morning light, soft and golden. Mood: calm, engaged, comfortable. Color palette: warm greens, soft yellows, child in bright clothing. Art style: warm lifestyle photography, editorial feel. Leave clear empty space in the bottom third for text overlay.

#### Article Hero (1200x630)
**Prompt:** A wide scene showing two children at a kitchen table doing light activities — one reading, one working with blocks or measuring cups. A parent is nearby. Natural light through a window. Mood: productive, relaxed, summer morning. Color palette: warm whites, soft kitchen tones, colorful books and activities. Art style: clean lifestyle photography. Leave clear empty space in the bottom third for text overlay.

---

research.facts:
Summer Slide Prevention
**Source 1:** scholastic.com/parents — "Summer Slide Statistics and Prevention"
- Average loss: 25–30% of school learning over summer
- Math experiences more severe loss than reading
- Younger children more affected
- Up to 48.5 points of achievement gap across 5 elementary years linked to cumulative summer loss
- Confidence: HIGH (Scholastic citing research)
**Source 2:** thethinkacademy.com — "How to Prevent Summer Slide in 2026"
- Tip: 20–30 min structured review 3–4 times/week
- Tip: daily reading at least 20 min; let kids choose material
- Tip: math in daily activities (cooking, shopping, counting)
- Confidence: MEDIUM (single source for specific tip details)
**Source 3:** mayoclinichealthsystem.org — "Tips for preventing 'summer slide'"
- Reading 20 min/day
- Consistent structured practice
- Confidence: HIGH (Mayo Clinic — authoritative medical source)
**Source 4:** scientificamerican.com — "Summer Learning Loss Happens, but Kids Quickly Recover"
- Confirms summer learning loss is real; recovery is possible with intervention
- Confidence: HIGH
**Local COS resources (from prior research):**
- PPYMCA: Week 2 Day Camp June 8–12 "Low-Key Famous Artist Week" (ppymca.org); Mini Camps June 15+ (confidence: HIGH — from ppymca.org)
- PPLD Summer Adventure: June 1–July 31, all ages, free, Beanstack app, 14 locations (confidence: HIGH — confirmed June 6 pipeline)
- Rock Ledge Ranch: June 3–14, 10am–5pm (confidence: MEDIUM — visitcos.com)
**Freshness justification:** Summer just started (Colorado Springs schools typically end late May/early June). Seasonal and evergreen relevance. Tips grounded in ongoing national research.

stories.title: Rock Ledge Ranch Closes This Saturday — Last Week for Hands-On History Fun
stories.angle: Rock Ledge Ranch Historic Site is in its final week of summer programming — open through Saturday, June 14, 10am–5pm. COS families can explore four eras of Colorado history in one visit: Native American trade, 1870s Rock Ledge House, 1880s pioneer homestead and garden, and Victorian-era games. The living history experience includes a working blacksmith, cornhusk doll making, and hands-on activities for all ages. Located at Garden of the Gods Park. Last chance this season.

x_posts.text:

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

stories.title: D49 Budget Vote Thursday — Student Success Center and New Programs on the Line
stories.angle: District 49's Board of Education meets Thursday, June 11 at Sand Creek High School to vote on the 2026–27 fiscal year budget. The budget includes funding for the Student Success Center opening August 2026 at the former Falcon Elementary of Technology — offering preschool Mon–Thu, Base49 before/after-school care, PEAK programs, and Elevates 18–21 services. This is a public meeting; the agenda is available at d49.org. Parents who want to weigh in can attend or submit comments ahead of the vote.
